channel.title: Frontion News
channel.description: Latest Headlines and Strategic Analysis of the Events Reshaping the World

item.title: Audio Captures Desperate Plea from Indian Tanker Captain Under Iranian Fire in Hormuz
item.description: Audio recordings reveal the moment an Indian oil tanker captain begged 'let me turn back' as Iranian forces opened fire in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran fired on two Indian-flagged vessels, prompting New Delhi to summon Tehran's ambassador in protest.

item.title: Netanyahu 'Shocked' Trump Prohibited Israel Strikes on Lebanon, Seeks Clarification
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu was reportedly shocked by Trump's public remarks prohibiting strikes on Lebanon, believing the statement exceeded the terms of the US-backed ceasefire. Israeli officials are seeking clarification from Washington.

item.title: Five Loaded Qatari LNG Vessels Approach Strait of Hormuz as Closure Looms
item.description: Five vessels loaded with liquefied natural gas from Qatar's Ras Laffan terminal are approaching the Strait of Hormuz, ship-tracking data showed. The vessels face uncertainty as Iran reimposed restrictions on the vital waterway.

item.title: WFP Warns Over Half of Haiti's Population Faces Worsening Hunger Crisis
item.description: The World Food Programme warned that more than 5.8 million Haitians — approximately 52% of the country's population — are facing acute hunger as the crisis-wracked Caribbean nation struggles with violence and political instability.

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Says 'Not in My Interest' to Debate Trump, Will Keep Preaching Peace
item.description: Pope Leo XIV said it is 'not in my interest at all' to debate President Trump about the war in Iran but that he will continue preaching the Gospel message of peace. The Pope spoke to reporters during his Africa tour in Angola.

item.title: US-Iran Deal Still Far from Reach as Major Gaps Remain on Enrichment
item.description: Analysis suggests a US-Iran deal remains distant despite ongoing negotiations. Major gaps persist on uranium enrichment levels, while Iran uses Hormuz leverage to push for security guarantees and sanctions relief.

item.title: IMF Warns Global Economy on Brink of Recession Amid Wars and Energy Crisis
item.description: The International Monetary Fund issued a stark warning that the global economy is 'threatened with being thrown off course' due to ongoing conflicts and soaring energy prices. The report highlights growing stagflation risks as the Iran war disrupts oil supplies and markets worldwide.

item.title: US Lawmaker Launches Investigation into Jared Kushner's Business Ties and Diplomatic Role
item.description: Democratic Representative Jamie Raskin opened a 'sweeping' investigation into President Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner over potential conflicts of interest. Kushner has been mediating Ukraine-Russia and Iran negotiations while running a private equity firm with potential financial stakes in the outcomes.

item.title: CENTCOM Reveals 13 US Troops Killed, Nearly 400 Wounded in Iran War
item.description: The US Central Command disclosed that 13 American troops have been killed and nearly 400 wounded in the Iran conflict. CENTCOM spokesman Tim Hawkins said 354 of the wounded have returned to duty, providing the first comprehensive casualty count since the war began.

item.title: HSBC and Standard Chartered Linked to Multimillion-Pound Iran Money Laundering Scheme
item.description: Two of Britain's largest banks have become embroiled in allegations of a multimillion-pound scheme to launder money for Iran. The allegations come as Western nations maintain strict sanctions on Tehran amid the ongoing conflict over the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: UK Startup to Test Acoustic Sensor System Against Russian Drones in Ukraine
item.description: British defense startup Arcani plans to test its HARK acoustic sensor system in Ukraine, marking another instance of Western defense technology being deployed in the conflict. The passive acoustic reconnaissance kit is designed to detect hostile drones and incoming threats.

item.title: US Forces Turn Back 23 Iranian Vessels as Naval Blockade Tightens
item.description: The US military reported that 23 ships have complied with orders to turn back toward Iran since the American blockade of Iranian ports began. The enforcement action is part of the 'Economic Fury' campaign to pressure Tehran over the Strait of Hormuz closure.

item.title: Trump Signs Executive Order Speeding Psychedelic Drug Reviews for PTSD Treatment
item.description: President Trump directed his administration to expedite reviews of psychedelic drugs, including ibogaine, for treating mental health conditions. The order follows advocacy from veterans groups and conservative lawmakers seeking alternatives for PTSD treatment.

item.title: Trump Organization Plans 70-Story Skyscraper in Tbilisi, Georgia
item.description: The Trump Organization and partners plan to build a 70-story 'Trump Tower' in Georgia's capital, the Wall Street Journal reported. The project comes as President Trump faces scrutiny over potential conflicts between his business interests and foreign policy decisions.

item.title: Venezuela's Machado Says 'No Regrets' About Giving Nobel Prize to Trump
item.description: Exiled opposition leader María Corina Machado defended her decision to symbolically gift her Nobel Peace Prize to Trump, calling him a leader who 'risked the lives of his country's citizens' for Venezuela's freedom. She remains in contact with the Trump administration about returning to Venezuela.

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Challenges Angola's Leaders to End Africa's Exploitation
item.description: During his Africa tour, Pope Leo XIV called on Angola's leaders to 'break the cycles of interests' that have plundered and exploited Africa, blasting 'despots and tyrants' who make false promises. He dismissed media narratives of a rift with Trump over the Iran war.

item.title: India's Defence Minister Reviews West Asia Situation Amid Hormuz Crisis
item.description: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh chaired a meeting in New Delhi to assess India's preparedness as the Strait of Hormuz crisis escalates. The review comes after Iran fired on two Indian tankers and India approved a $1.4 billion maritime insurance pool.

item.title: Trump Convenes Situation Room Briefing as Iran Chokes Off World Oil Supply
item.description: President Trump called an emergency meeting with his inner circle, including JD Vance, Marco Rubio, and Pete Hegseth, to discuss the Strait of Hormuz crisis. The briefing came after Iran reimposed restrictions on the vital waterway.

item.title: Trump, IRS in Talks to Settle President's $10 Billion Lawsuit Over Leaked Tax Returns
item.description: Lawyers for Donald Trump and the IRS are negotiating a settlement of the president's $10 billion lawsuit against the tax agency for leaking his tax returns to media outlets in 2019 and 2020. The case has drawn attention to privacy protections for taxpayer information.

item.title: Japan Embraces Nuclear Power Post-Fukushima as AI Drives Energy Demand
item.description: Japan is rapidly moving to restart nuclear reactors as artificial intelligence increases electricity demand and foreign wars reshape energy security thinking. The country is reconsidering its post-Fukushima nuclear phase-out as it faces growing power needs.

item.title: Iran's Resistance Offers Lessons for Taiwan, Security Analysts Say
item.description: Iran's ability to prevent a decisive US victory despite inferior weapons has implications for Taiwan's defense strategy. Analysts note that decentralized command, dispersed weapons, and large drone/missile stockpiles helped Iran close the Strait of Hormuz and withstand strikes.

item.title: Kyiv Gunman Was Russian-Born, Zelenskyy Calls Shooting 'Act of Terrorism'
item.description: Ukraine's security service labeled the Kyiv supermarket attack as terrorism after the 58-year-old gunman was identified as Russian-born. The shooter had a valid weapons permit, prior criminal record, and had lived in the Donetsk region before killing six and wounding 14.

item.title: Trump Says China's Xi Is 'Very Happy' About Hormuz Status Ahead of China Visit
item.description: President Trump said Chinese President Xi Jinping was 'very happy' about developments with the Strait of Hormuz and reiterated he looks forward to their meeting in China next month. The trip was delayed from March due to the Iran conflict.

item.title: Russia Strikes Ukrainian Port and Power Grid, Ukraine Hits Russian Oil Facilities
item.description: Russian attacks overnight damaged port infrastructure in Ukraine's Odesa region and caused blackouts for 380,000 consumers in the north. Ukraine struck back with drone attacks on oil refineries in Samara, a Crimea oil depot, and a Baltic Sea petroleum port.

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Condemns 'Despots and Tyrants' Exploiting Africa During Angola Visit
item.description: Pope Leo XIV sharply decried the exploitation of natural resources in Africa during a visit to Angola, blasting 'despots and tyrants' who guarantee wealth but deliver suffering. He also played down any rift with Trump over the Iran war.
